                {"version": "1", "schema": "unknown_schema"},

                    },

                id="packages-item-type",

                        }

                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "git" / "config.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_validate_rejects_invalid_6')
config_payload = {'os': {'name': 'linux', 'package_manager': 'pacman'}, 'schema': 'module', 'version': '1'}
expected_error_parts = ('os.package_manager', 'brew', 'apt', 'yum')

    @pytest.mark.error
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("config_payload", "expected_error_parts"),
        [
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1"},
                ("schema", "required"),
                id="schema-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "unknown_schema"},
                ("schema", "unknown_schema"),
                id="schema-unrecognized",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": 1},
                ("schema", "string"),
                id="schema-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": "linux"},
                ("os", "object"),
                id="os-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {}},
                ("os.name", "required"),
                id="os-name-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "os": {"name": "windows"}},
                ("os.name", "macos", "linux"),
                id="os-name-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {"name": "linux", "package_manager": "pacman"},
                },
                ("os.package_manager", "brew", "apt", "yum"),
                id="package-manager-invalid",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": "curl",
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages", "list"),
                id="packages-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": [1],
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages[0]", "string"),
                id="packages-item-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "packages": [""],
                    },
                },
                ("os.packages[0]", "non-empty"),
                id="packages-item-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {
                        "name": "linux",
                        "package_manager": "apt",
                        "applications": "firefox",
                    },
                },
                ("os.applications", "list"),
                id="applications-type",
            ),

            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",

                },
                ("actions[0].type", "string"),
                id="action-type-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

                },
                ("actions[0].type", "move"),
                id="action-type-unknown",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [{"type": "run"}]},

            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

                id="action-source-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

            ),
            pytest.param(

                    "version": "1",

            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

            ),
            pytest.param(

                    "version": "1",

                id="destination-string",
            ),

                        }

                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),

    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:

                id="elevated-type",

                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-link",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-copy",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a", "destination": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "string"),
                id="destination-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].hidden", "boolean"),
                id="hidden-type",

                    "version": "1",

                            "elevated": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:

                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "string"),
                id="action-source-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": ""}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "non-empty"),
                id="action-source-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": "/etc/passwd"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "module-relative"),
                id="action-source-relative",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "link", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-link",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "required"),
                id="destination-required-copy",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "copy", "source": "a", "destination": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].destination", "string"),
                id="destination-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "hidden": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].hidden", "boolean"),
                id="hidden-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "a",
                            "destination": "~/a",
                            "elevated": "yes",
                        }
                    ],
                },
                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "git" / "config.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

                {
                    "version": "1",

                    "actions": [{"type": 1, "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "string"),
                id="action-type-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,

        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],

                    "schema": "module",
                    "os": {"name": "linux", "applications": ["firefox"]},
                },
                ("os.package_manager", "required", "applications"),
                id="package-manager-required-with-apps",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": "copy"},
                ("actions", "list"),
                id="actions-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [1]},
                ("actions[0]", "object"),
                id="action-item-type",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

                    "actions": [{"source": "file.sh"}],
                },

                id="action-type-required",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": 1, "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "string"),
                id="action-type-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "move", "source": "file.sh"}],
                },
                ("actions[0].type", "move"),
                id="action-type-unknown",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {"version": "1", "schema": "module", "actions": [{"type": "run"}]},
                ("actions[0].source", "required"),
                id="action-source-required",

            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",

                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": 1}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "string"),
                id="action-source-string",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                {
                    "version": "1",
                    "schema": "module",
                    "actions": [{"type": "run", "source": ""}],
                },
                ("actions[0].source", "non-empty"),
                id="action-source-empty",
            ),
            pytest.param(

                id="action-source-relative",

                id="destination-string",

                            "destination": "~/a",

                ("actions[0].elevated", "boolean"),
                id="elevated-type",

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_fields(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, object],
        expected_error_parts: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:

            },
        )

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_2.py:335:

payload = {'modules': []}, module_name = 'dock'

    def _actions_for_module(
        payload: dict[str, Any], module_name: str
    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        for module in payload["modules"]:
            if module["name"] == module_name:
                return list(module["actions"])
        msg = f"module '{module_name}' not found"
>       raise AssertionError(msg)

        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:

            tmp_path / "system" / "preferences.yaml",

                [

    def test_plan_topological_sort(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Plans modules in dependency order with alphabetical tie-breaking."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["c"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "d",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["b"])},
        )

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_merges_cross_config_deps(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Merges depends_on declarations from multiple schemas in one module."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "networking",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "tools",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "dev",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["core", "networking", "core"],
                ),
                "preferences.yaml": preferences_schema(
                    [
                        {
                            "name": "Show status bar",
                            "domain": "com.apple.finder",
                            "key": "ShowStatusBar",
                            "value": True,
                            "value_type": "bool",
                            "apply_command": (
                                "defaults write com.apple.finder "
                                "ShowStatusBar -bool true"
                            ),
                        }
                    ],
                    depends_on=["tools", "core"],
                ),
            },
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_environments_shapes(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        config_payload: dict[str, Any],
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects invalid environments payload shapes and field-level path errors."""
        write_yaml(tmp_path / "dev" / "environments.yaml", config_payload)
    
        result = run_validate(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        ("depends_on", "fragment"),
        [
            pytest.param("core", "depends_on", id="depends-on-not-list"),

            pytest.param([""], "depends_on[0]", id="depends-on-item-empty"),
            pytest.param([None], "depends_on[0]", id="depends-on-item-null"),
        ],
    )
    def test_validate_rejects_invalid_depends_on(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        depends_on: object,
        fragment: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Rejects depends_on when it is not a non-empty-string dependency list."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "dev" / "module.yaml",
            {
                "version": "1",
                "schema": "module",

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_deduplicates_deps0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_deduplicates_deps(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Deduplicates repeated dependencies before ordering modules."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["core", "core", "core"],
                )
            },
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)

            "core",
            "app",
        ]

E         -     'app',
E         - ]

_________________ test_plan_file_not_found_before_circular_dep _________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_file_not_found_befor1')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_file_not_found_before_circular_dep(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Prioritizes file_not_found before checking circular dependencies."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "missing.txt",
                            "destination": "/tmp/missing.txt",
                        }
                    ],
                    depends_on=["b"],
                )
            },
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["c"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["a"])},
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 1
        error = parse_stderr_json(result)
>       assert error["error"] == "file_not_found"
E       AssertionError: assert 'circular_dependency' == 'file_not_found'
E         
E         - file_not_found
E         + circular_dependency

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_3.py:1756: AssertionError
____________________ test_plan_diamond_dependency_ordering _____________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_diamond_dependency_o0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_diamond_dependency_ordering(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Orders modules correctly in a diamond dependency graph."""
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "a",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "b",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["a"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "c",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["a"])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "d",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[], depends_on=["b", "c"])
            },
        )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path)
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
>       assert [m["name"] for m in payload["modules"]] == ["a", "b", "c", "d"]
E       AssertionError: assert [] == ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd']
E         
E         Right contains 4 more items, first extra item: 'a'
E         
E         Full diff:
E         + []
E         - [
E         -     'a',
E         -     'b',
E         -     'c',
E         -     'd',

        else:

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_4.py:573: AssertionError
__________________ test_plan_profile_dedup_inheritance_chain ___________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_plan_profile_dedup_inheri0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_plan_profile_dedup_inheritance_chain(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Plan deduplicates inherited modules across transitive profile hierarchy."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "base": {"modules": ["core", "dev-tools"]},
                    "work": {
                        "extends": "base",
                        "modules": ["dev-tools", "work-apps"],
                    },
                    "full": {"extends": "work", "modules": ["core", "media"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        for module_name in ["core", "dev-tools", "work-apps", "media"]:
            write_module(
                tmp_path,
                module_name,
                configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
            )
    
        result = run_plan(entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--profile", "full")
    
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        payload = parse_stdout_json(result)
>       assert _module_names(payload) == [
            "core",
            "dev-tools",
            "work-apps",
            "media",
        ]
E       AssertionError: assert [] == ['core', 'dev-tools', 'work-apps', 'media']
E         
E         Right contains 4 more items, first extra item: 'core'
E         
E         Full diff:
E         + []
E         - [
E         -     'core',
E         -     'dev-tools',
E         -     'work-apps',
E         -     'media',
E         - ]

.evaluation_tests/test_checkpoint_4.py:609: AssertionError
_________________ test_list_profiles_invalid_inherited_modules _________________

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_list_profiles_invalid_inh0')

    @pytest.mark.error
    def test_list_profiles_invalid_inherited_modules(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """List-profiles reports invalid_profile when inherited module definitions are missing."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "zbase": {"modules": ["core", "missing-base"]},
                    "alpha": {
                        "extends": "zbase",
                        "modules": ["missing-work"],
                    },
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",

            "app",

        )

        )

        )

            tmp_path,
            "shell",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )

            "macos",
            "--output",
            "out",
            "--release",
        )

            pytest.param("data.yml", "name: cfg\n", id="yml"),
        ],
    )
    def test_build_copies_referenced_data_files_even_with_config_like_extensions(
        entrypoint_argv,

    ) -> None:

                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": file_name,
                            "destination": str(destination),
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={file_name: content},
        )

        )

tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_p0')
monkeypatch = <_pytest.monkeypatch.MonkeyPatch object at 0x7fdefc72c440>
os_name = 'macos', package_manager = 'brew', package_name = 'coreutils'
application_name = 'firefox', allowed_prefixes = ('brew ',)

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        (
            "os_name",
            "package_manager",
            "package_name",
            "application_name",
            "allowed_prefixes",
        ),
        [
            pytest.param(
                "macos",
                "brew",
                "coreutils",
                "firefox",
                ("brew ",),
                id="brew",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "apt",
                "curl",
                "vim",
                ("apt ", "apt-get "),
                id="apt",
            ),
            pytest.param(
                "linux",
                "yum",
                "wget",
                "htop",
                ("yum ",),
                id="yum",
            ),
        ],
    )
    def test_install_script_executes_package_and_app_commands_for_each_manager(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
        monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
        os_name: str,
        package_manager: str,
        package_name: str,
        application_name: str,
        allowed_prefixes: tuple[str, ...],
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes package and application installation via OS manager commands."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "invocations.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        for command_name in ["brew", "apt", "apt-get", "yum"]:
            command_path = fake_bin / command_name
            command_path.write_text(
                "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
                f'printf \'%s\\n\' "{command_name} $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            )
            command_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        monkeypatch.setenv("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG", str(log_path))
        monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}")
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    os_name=os_name,
                    package_manager=package_manager,
                    packages=[package_name],
                    applications=[application_name],
                    actions=[],
                )
            },
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", os_name, "--output", "out"
        )
        assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
        package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
>           tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
        )

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_uses_sudo_for_elevated_file_and_run_actions(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Elevated installer actions perform privileged operations under sudo."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "sudo.log"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        sudo_cmd = fake_bin / "sudo"
        sudo_cmd.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "sudo $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            'exec "$@"\n'
        )
        sudo_cmd.chmod(0o755)
    
        destination_dir = tmp_path / "dest"
        run_marker = tmp_path / "ran.txt"

            tmp_path,
            "dev",

                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "b.txt",
                            "destination": str(destination_dir / "b.txt"),
                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "run",
                            "source": "setup.sh",
                            "elevated": True,
                        },
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={
                "a.txt": "a",
                "b.txt": "b",
                "setup.sh": f"printf ran > '{run_marker}'\n",
            },
        )

            "RIG_INVOCATION_LOG": str(log_path),

        }
        old_log = os.environ.get("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG")
        old_path = os.environ.get("PATH")
        os.environ.update(monkeypatch_env)

                entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
            )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_dry_run_do0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_dry_run_does_not_execute_actions(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Installer --dry-run does not execute actions or modify destination files."""
        home_dir = tmp_path / "home"
        home_dir.mkdir()
        ran_marker = tmp_path / "ran.txt"
        pref_marker = tmp_path / "pref-applied.txt"
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "copy",
                            "source": "copy.txt",
                            "destination": "~/.config/rig/copied.txt",
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "link",
                            "source": "link.txt",
                            "destination": "~/.config/rig/linked.txt",
                        },
                        {
                            "type": "run",
                            "source": "setup.sh",
                        },
                    ]
                ),
                "preferences.yaml": preferences_schema(
                    [
                        {
                            "name": "Apply marker",
                            "domain": "com.example.rig",
                            "key": "marker",
                            "value": True,
                            "value_type": "bool",
                            "apply_command": f"printf applied > '{pref_marker}'",
                        }
                    ]
                ),
            },
            files={
                "copy.txt": "copy",
                "link.txt": "link",
                "setup.sh": f"printf ran > '{ran_marker}'\n",
            },
        )
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--output",
            "out",
            home_dir=home_dir,
        )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_execution_3')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_execution_run_action_preserves_script_interpreter(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Run action preserves executable script interpreter configuration."""
        marker = tmp_path / "python-run-executed.txt"
        module_dir = write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[
                        {
                            "type": "run",
                            "source": "setup.py",
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
            files={
                "setup.py": (
                    "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n"
                    "from pathlib import Path\n"
                    f"Path({marker.as_posix()!r}).write_text('ok')\n"
                )
            },
        )
        (module_dir / "setup.py").chmod(0o755)
    
        build_result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
        )
        assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
        package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
>           tmp_path, parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"]
                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
        )

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_profile_resolves_in0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_build_profile_resolves_inheritance_and_auto_includes_dependencies(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Profile inheritance resolves ancestor modules before building package modules."""
        write_yaml(
            tmp_path / "manifest.yaml",
            manifest_schema(
                {
                    "minimal": {"modules": ["core"]},
                    "work": {"extends": "minimal", "modules": ["app"]},
                }
            ),
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "core",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "shell",
            configs={"module.yaml": module_schema(actions=[])},
        )
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "app",
            configs={
                "module.yaml": module_schema(
                    actions=[],
                    depends_on=["shell"],
                )
            },
        )
    
        result = run_build(
            entrypoint_argv,
            tmp_path,
            "--os",
            "macos",
            "--profile",
            "work",
            "--output",
            "out",
        )
    
>       assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
E       AssertionError: {"error": "invalid_profile", "details": {"profile": "work", "missing_modules": ["app", "core"]}}
E         
E       assert 1 == 0
E        +  where 1 = CompletedProcess(args=['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py', 'build', '/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_build_profile_resolves_in0', '--os', 'macos', '--profile', 'work', '--output', 'out'], returncode=1, stdout='', stderr='{"error": "invalid_profile", "details": {"profile": "work", "missing_modules": ["app", "core"]}}\n').returncode

entrypoint_argv = ['uv', 'run', '--project', '/workspace', '/workspace/rig.py']
tmp_path = PosixPath('/tmp/pytest-of-agent/pytest-0/test_install_script_executes_r0')

    @pytest.mark.functionality
    def test_install_script_executes_runtime_plugin_and_virtual_env_steps_for_pyenv(
        entrypoint_argv,
        tmp_path: Path,
    ) -> None:
        """Installer executes pyenv runtime plugin and virtualenv creation steps."""
        log_path = tmp_path / "runtime.log"
        pyenv_root = tmp_path / "pyenv-root"
        fake_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
        fake_bin.mkdir()
    
        pyenv_path = fake_bin / "pyenv"
        pyenv_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "pyenv $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        pyenv_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        plugin_path = fake_bin / "pyenv-virtualenv"
        plugin_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'plugin_dir="${PYENV_ROOT:-$HOME/.pyenv}/plugins/pyenv-virtualenv"\n'
            'if [[ "${1:-}" == "install" ]]; then\n'
            '  mkdir -p "$plugin_dir"\n'
            "fi\n"
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "pyenv-virtualenv $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        plugin_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        git_path = fake_bin / "git"
        git_path.write_text(
            "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n"
            'dest="$3"\n'
            'parent="$(dirname "$dest")"\n'
            'if [[ ! -d "$parent" ]]; then\n'
            '  printf \'%s\\n\' "git-missing-parent $dest" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
            "  exit 88\n"
            "fi\n"
            'mkdir -p "$dest"\n'
            'printf \'%s\\n\' "git $*" >> "$RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"\n'
        )
        git_path.chmod(0o755)
    
        write_module(
            tmp_path,
            "dev",
            configs={
                "environments.yaml": environments_schema(
                    [
                        {
                            "language": "python",
                            "versions": ["3.11.2"],
                            "manager": {
                                "name": "pyenv",
                                "plugins": [
                                    {
                                        "name": "pyenv-virtualenv",
                                        "virtual_environments": [
                                            {
                                                "version": "3.11.2",
                                                "name": "default-3.11",
                                            }
                                        ],
                                    }
                                ],
                            },
                        }
                    ]
                )
            },
        )
    
        old_log = os.environ.get("RIG_INVOCATION_LOG")
        old_path = os.environ.get("PATH")
        old_pyenv_root = os.environ.get("PYENV_ROOT")
        os.environ["RIG_INVOCATION_LOG"] = str(log_path)
        os.environ["PATH"] = f"{fake_bin}:{os.environ['PATH']}"
        os.environ["PYENV_ROOT"] = str(pyenv_root)
    
        try:
            build_result = run_build(
                entrypoint_argv, tmp_path, "--os", "macos", "--output", "out"
            )
            assert build_result.returncode == 0, build_result.stderr
            package_dir = _resolve_reported_path(
                tmp_path,
>               parse_stdout_json(build_result)["packages"][0]["directory"],
                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
